metrics.title: FY2026 Guidance
metrics.label: ~45% YoY growth — Mounjaro +110% YoY in Q4 2025

metrics.title: Foundayo Approved
metrics.label: FDA-approved oral GLP-1 pill — 1,390 debut-week Rx; $1.5–2.8B 2026 analyst est.

metrics.title: Retatrutide Ph.3
metrics.label: TRIUMPH-4 Phase 3 confirmed — 71.2 lbs avg loss; 7 more Ph.3 readouts in 2026

moat.description: Dominant GLP-1 franchise with patent protection into the late 2030s, reinforced by a deep clinical pipeline and manufacturing scale advantages.
moat.analysisSummary: Eli Lilly's moat is anchored in **scientific superiority and regulatory exclusivity** that cannot be replicated quickly:

analysisPoints.title: Tirzepatide Efficacy Superiority
analysisPoints.text: Mounjaro and Zepbound (tirzepatide) consistently outperform competing GLP-1 drugs in head-to-head efficacy data, with superior weight loss outcomes. This clinical evidence edge drives prescriber preference and formulary positioning that competitors must overcome with their own Phase 3 trial data — a multi-year barrier.

analysisPoints.title: Patent Wall Into the Late 2030s
analysisPoints.text: Lilly's CEO has confirmed tirzepatide compound patent protection extends 'into the back half of the 2030s' in major markets, contrasting sharply with Novo Nordisk's semaglutide facing patent expiry in select markets today. This 10+ year runway allows Lilly to compound its GLP-1 franchise revenues without near-term generic erosion.

analysisPoints.title: Manufacturing Moat at Scale
analysisPoints.text: Peptide manufacturing is extraordinarily complex; Lilly has invested billions into new manufacturing capacity globally. The expertise required to manufacture GLP-1 drugs at commercial scale — including sterile fill-finish and cold-chain logistics — represents a structural barrier that takes years for competitors to build.

growth.description: FY2025: $65.18B revenue (+44.7% YoY). Q4 2025: $19.29B (+42.6% YoY) — Mounjaro $7.41B (+110%), Zepbound $4.26B (+123%). FY2026 guidance $80–83B (~25% YoY). Foundayo (orforglipron oral GLP-1) FDA-approved April 1, 2026 — 1,390 debut-week Rx (vs Wegovy pill 3,071 in first 4 days); analyst 2026 revenue est. $1.5–2.8B, priced at $299/mo self-pay or $25/mo with commercial insurance. Retatrutide TRIUMPH-4 Phase 3 confirmed 28.7% body weight loss / 71.2 lbs avg at high dose — seven additional Ph.3 readouts expected in 2026; GlobalData projects $15.6B in 2031 peak sales. Q1 2026 earnings April 30 (est. $17.6B revenue, EPS $7.21).

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 90 (30%+ CAGR) + 5 oral GLP-1 TAM expansion (Foundayo FDA-approved April 1, opens injection-resistant patients) + 5 retatrutide next-gen catalyst (TRIUMPH-4 Phase 3 confirmed 28.7%/71.2 lbs; 7 more 2026 readouts) − 5 oral competition headwind (Wegovy pill led debut-week scripts 3,071 vs Foundayo 1,390; Structure Therapeutics aleniglipron Phase 2) − 5 pricing risk (Medicare Part D negotiation risk) = 90

drivers.name: Zepbound (obesity)
drivers.metric: $4.26B Q4 2025 (+123% YoY), launched Jan 2024 — still ramping

drivers.name: Foundayo (oral GLP-1) + Retatrutide
drivers.metric: Foundayo FDA-approved Apr 1, 2026 — 1,390 debut-week Rx; retatrutide TRIUMPH-4 Ph.3 confirmed 28.7%/71.2 lbs

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If Novo Nordisk's oral Wegovy pill sustains a >2:1 prescription lead over Foundayo through Q3 2026 AND Structure Therapeutics' aleniglipron Phase 3 outperforms orforglipron in 2027, LLY's oral GLP-1 market share falls below 25% and analyst 2028 EPS estimates decline 15%+

valuation.description: At ~$924 — 23% below the $1,200 base case and 19% off the 52-week high of $1,134 — LLY continues to trade at a discount despite clearing the biggest near-term catalyst risk: Foundayo (orforglipron) FDA-approved April 1, 2026. The persistent discount reflects early prescription competition from the Wegovy pill and investor caution ahead of the April 30 Q1 2026 earnings call. Analyst consensus target is ~$1,218, implying 32% upside from current levels.

peAnalysis.summary: At ~33× forward GAAP P/E with a PEG of ~0.7×, LLY remains significantly cheaper than its large-cap pharma peer average (~45×), despite Foundayo's FDA approval on April 1 adding a third major commercial product to the portfolio. The sub-1.0 PEG on ~45% EPS growth is characteristic of a mispriced compounder — the market is paying a Wegovy-competition discount on a franchise that now holds both the leading injectable (Zepbound/Mounjaro) and an approved oral (Foundayo). The 49× trailing versus 33× forward P/E gap confirms accelerating earnings, not multiple expansion.

bear.description: Foundayo adoption disappoints as Wegovy pill leads on scripts, pricing pressure erodes net revenue, and a retatrutide late Phase 3 setback removes the next-gen premium.
points: Novo Nordisk's oral Wegovy pill sustains a >2:1 prescription lead over Foundayo through 2026, capturing 30%+ of new oral GLP-1 prescriptions; Structure Therapeutics' aleniglipron Phase 3 outperforms Foundayo in 2027, fragmenting LLY's oral market share below 20%
U.S. Medicare/Medicaid Part D GLP-1 net pricing negotiations cut realised prices by 25%+ before 2028, compressing revenue growth below 15% and pushing FY2027 revenue to ~$90B vs. consensus $110B+

Retatrutide late Phase 3 readouts fail to maintain the TRIUMPH-4 superiority across all indications — settling at ~20% mean weight loss — eliminating the 'best-in-class next-gen' premium and leaving LLY without a differentiated asset beyond Zepbound

base.description: Foundayo gains adoption momentum closing the Wegovy script gap by Q3 2026, retatrutide's remaining Phase 3 readouts confirm superiority, and FY2026 revenue closes at the high end of guidance.
points: Foundayo narrows the Wegovy pill prescription gap to <1.5:1 by Q3 2026 as patients and prescribers favour its no food/water restriction dosing; LLY secures telehealth distribution with Hims or a rival platform, capturing 25%+ of new oral GLP-1 Rx

Retatrutide's remaining 2026 Phase 3 readouts maintain 22%+ mean body weight loss across indications, with FDA submission by end of 2026 — establishing LLY as the only company with best-in-class oral and injectable next-gen GLP-1s
Kisunla (donanemab) exceeds $500M FY2026 revenue on growing Alzheimer's formulary access, and FY2026 revenue closes at the high end of $80–83B guidance with non-GAAP EPS ~$35

bull.description: Retatrutide becomes the best-in-class weight-loss drug confirmed by Phase 3 data, Foundayo accelerates to mainstream oral adoption, and Kisunla reaches blockbuster scale.
points: Retatrutide Phase 3 breadth (TRIUMPH-4 confirmed 28.7%/71.2 lbs avg) across all 2026 readouts leads to FDA approval by mid-2027; GlobalData $15.6B 2031 sales projection realised, generating >$10B revenue by 2029

Foundayo penetrates 30%+ of the oral obesity market within 3 years of launch, as no food/water restriction advantage drives patient preference over Wegovy pill — doubling the addressable patient pool vs. injection-only therapies
Kisunla (donanemab) reaches $3B+ annual revenue by 2028 as Alzheimer's diagnosis rates improve and reimbursement expands globally, reducing GLP-1 revenue concentration below 60%

learnedInterfaces.note: N/A — physicians prescribe drugs based on clinical evidence and patient outcomes, not UI familiarity; interface learning costs do not apply to pharmaceuticals.

businessLogic.note: Lilly's GLP-1 peptide synthesis, formulation IP, and cold-chain manufacturing know-how represent complex proprietary processes that competitors cannot decode from reverse engineering alone.

publicDataAccess.note: N/A — Lilly's competitive moat does not rely on controlling access to a public data source; not applicable to the pharmaceutical business model.

talentScarcity.note: World-class endocrinologists, peptide chemists, and regulatory affairs specialists are scarce globally; Lilly's 140-year reputation and compensation attract the best pharma talent disproportionately.

bundling.note: N/A — pharmaceutical drugs are not sold in software-style bundles; individual drug approvals and prescriptions are the unit of sale.

proprietaryData.note: Lilly holds decades of real-world clinical trial data for tirzepatide across thousands of patients — outcomes data that grows with every prescription and feeds future drug development and label expansions.

regulatoryLockIn.note: Mounjaro, Zepbound, and now Foundayo (FDA-approved April 1, 2026) each carry independent FDA approvals, EU/global regulatory clearances, GMP manufacturing certifications, and payer formulary placements — three interlocking regulatory moats that compound switching barriers across both injectable and oral GLP-1 channels.

networkEffects.note: Growing physician experience with tirzepatide creates a prescribing flywheel — more patient success stories build prescriber confidence and peer recommendations. Foundayo's April 2026 launch extends this network into the oral GLP-1 segment, deepening the flywheel across both injectable and pill modalities.

transactionEmbedding.note: Mounjaro and Zepbound are embedded in major pharmacy benefit formularies, employer health plans, and Medicare Part D — removing them faces enormous patient, physician, and political resistance.

systemOfRecord.note: N/A — Lilly is a drug manufacturer, not a system-of-record platform; this moat type does not apply to the pharmaceutical industry.

tenMoats.verdict: Lilly's core moat — patent-protected biologics with regulatory approval — is structurally immune to AI disruption, and AI drug discovery tools actually accelerate Lilly's pipeline advantage by compressing early-stage discovery timelines. The company is a net beneficiary of AI adoption, using it to identify novel targets and optimize clinical trials faster than underfunded competitors.
